objective: Authorize Argo execution of the Clotho Phase 1 implementation plan v11 (content address sha256:f5d9cd52f12ec9abb4c613c469437d0079af7fdf249c5a842f94c451d55fc30c; released by The Eye via PR #94, merge commit 7c200e5643043d72578e617dc7e8650370c775e6). The plan builds clotho/, an advisory zero-dependency knowledge-graph package proven by weaving TELOS itself. Approve ONLY if the plan is implementation-ready and consistent with its governing spec (v2.8), the repository's trust model (fail-closed, closed sets, spine read-only, advisory-only Clotho), and its own decisions and exit criteria.

blockers: codex has required edits: Route a specification amendment through The Eye and revise D23/D30 and accepted risk 18 with enforceable replacement text covering present loader-construction and executable-code routes in both directions. Implement fail-closed handling and synthetic rejection tests for, at minimum, aliased createRequire outside Clotho, outside use of process.getBuiltinModule to acquire a loader, Function/eval-family generated import or require code, and computed or aliased variants that the scanner cannot classify safely; unsupported loader-relevant syntax must be fatal rather than ignored.; Require a validated manifest for threadsOf and blastRadius, as already required for expected-kind gap claims, or conservatively report all relevant producer coverage as unknown when no manifest is supplied. Do not return an empty coverageUnknown array when coverage was not established. Update interfaces and missing-manifest tests accordingly.; In weave.mjs, derive each weaver and orchestrator accepted module-load closure from the actual on-disk bytes with the shared classifier and resolver, compare it exactly with the committed inventories, and verify that the hashes used in implementation_refs and orchestrator_refs still match immediately before close/publication. Abort on any drift. Add driver tests where an on-disk source gains a re-export, literal dynamic import, or accepted require-style edge absent from its inventory and prove that no ledger is closed or published.; Before appending any weaver result, require every edge to have asserted_by exactly equal to the invoked weaver id and assertion_status equal to deterministic-extraction; likewise require warning.weaver to equal that id. Reject cross-weaver, human, or model attribution from a weaver result and add driver-level tests for those cases.

codex has hard stops: The advisory-boundary scanner does not cover current executable loader-evasion routes, so it cannot establish the claimed inbound or outbound isolation.; The query API treats an omitted coverage manifest as an empty coverage-unknown set for threadsOf and blastRadius, allowing incomplete results to appear complete.; The complete-weave driver does not derive and enforce module-load closure equality at publication time, allowing a changed worktree mechanism to publish provenance that omits newly reachable files.; The driver does not require each returned edge's asserted_by value to equal the weaver that produced it, permitting false mechanism attribution.

authorization.note: Fail-closed: see gate.blockers and seat decisions.
